brick: the WHOLE GQA CAUSAL LLAMA BLOCK runs on the M4 Max GPU — the real llama3.2 decoder block (GQA attention + RoPE + RMSNorm + SwiGLU), n_q query heads sharing n_kv<n_q KV heads, bit-exact with the proven Form recipe
author: Sema (Opus 4.8, autonomous sema-native-ml-walk)
north_star_rung: GPU driver-replacement / real-llama-on-GPU: the named next stone from 3zh (gqa-attn on GPU, #3587) — 'emit a WHOLE GQA llama block kernel (RMSNorm+proj+RoPE+GQA-attn+FFN, the GQA generalization of jte-llama-block-fwd-causal-msl #3376) and run on the M4 GPU'. 3zh put GQA *attention* on silicon; this puts the whole GQA *block* on silicon. The single-head causal block (#3376) becomes the n_q=n_kv=1 special case of a kernel that runs the real 24->8 grouping shape.
lane: NEW-GPU-kernel + CPU-mirror + bit-exact gate (the 3m/3s/3u/3y/3zh lane). Design (the GQA projection widths — k/v to the smaller n_kv*HD; the per-head grouped causal attention loop kvh=h/group; the parity insight that passing the full 4x4 wk/wv and reading only the first KVD rows == the recipe's tb-seq-headslice(kvh=0); the cfg10==base-10000 RoPE equivalence from band claim 1) was the cost; both proofs were seconds because the body had ALREADY built the single-head GPU block template (jte-llama-block-fwd-causal-msl #3376) AND the proven four-way GQA block recipe (llama-gqa-block.fk, lgqa-block-causal four-way 15).
read_the_body: Almost authored a from-scratch GQA block recipe — then found form/form-stdlib/llama-gqa-block.fk ALREADY exists with lgqa-block-causal proven FOUR-WAY 15 (tests/llama-gqa-block-causal-band.fk). The fp64 reference was DONE. The real gap was precisely the GPU lowering: no emit band, no audit script for the GQA block. The 18th time read-the-body paid off — the hard part (the recipe + numerics + four-way proof) was already standing.
what_changed: form/form-stdlib/jit-tensor-emit.fk: added jte-gqa-llama-block-fwd-causal-msl (+ jte-gqa-blk-sig/off/proj/rope/attn-causal/out) — emits lgqa-block-causal as ONE Metal kernel. The GQA generalization of jte-llama-block-fwd-causal-msl: q projects to dq=n_q*HD, k/v to the SMALLER KVD=n_kv*HD; each query head h dots its HD-wide q-slice (h*HD) against KV head (h/group)'s HD-wide k/v-slice over the causal prefix t<s+1u, context concatenated to n_q*HD. RMSNorm1/RMSNorm2 + SwiGLU FFN are jte-llama-blk-rms1/rms2/ffn VERBATIM (same scratch offsets). group=1 (n_kv=n_q) IS the single-head block per head (core-abstraction-first, no parallel path). RoPE base-10000 = cfg10 special case of lgqa's rope-scaled.

form/form-stdlib/tests/llama-gqa-block-fwd-causal-emit-band.fk: new emit band — the full emitted f32 MSL for form_gqa_llama_block_causal_fwd_f32 equals the canonical 7259-byte text (the cross-kernel byte-identity anchor), emission is a function of the name, deterministic. Generated by splitting the canonical bytes on the kernel name (no hand-transcription). Verdict 7.

scripts/metal_gqa_llama_block_audit.sh: the on-hardware witness — emits the kernel, compiles mathMode-safe (IEEE, no fast-math contraction), runs on the M4 Max GPU over llama-gqa-block-causal-band's claim-8 fixture (the real GQA grouping: 2 query heads share 1 KV head), gates GPU==CPU-fp32-mirror bit-exact AND GPU tracks the fp64 recipe within fp32 epsilon.
scripts/INDEX.md: regenerated to list the new audit script.

emit_band_four_way.result: 1 ok, 0 divergent — Go/Rust/TS/fkwu emit the 7259-byte GQA block MSL byte-identical; fourth arm crossed

recipe_four_way.pr: tracked four-way at the recipe altitude — lgqa-block-causal proven against the libm fp64 reference
recipe_four_way.note: this GPU brick is the silicon realization of that proven recipe

on_gpu.kernel: form_gqa_llama_block_causal_fwd_f32 (7259 bytes, every byte authored by jte-gqa-llama-block-fwd-causal-msl)
on_gpu.fixture: llama-gqa-block-causal-band claim 8: S=2 tokens, d=4, n_q=2 query heads, n_kv=1 KV head, HD=2, scale 1/sqrt(2), RoPE base-10000 (cfg10) — both query heads 0,1 -> kv0 (the h/group mapping llama3.2 runs at 24 -> 8)
on_gpu.gate_1_parity: GPU fp32 == CPU fp32 mirror BIT-FOR-BIT (max|y_gpu - y_cpu| = 0.0)
on_gpu.gate_2_recipe: GPU fp32 tracks fp64 lgqa-block-causal *1e6 within fp32 epsilon (max|Δ| = 0.4)
on_gpu.transcendentals: softmax exp / SiLU sigmoid = the recipe's OWN fexp Taylor; RoPE sin/cos/pow = trig.fk's own Taylor; RMSNorm = biased mean-square + 50-step Newton sqrt — never Metal's transcendentals. Every dot is the downward right-fold split through a named temporary (no fma contraction).

honest_floor: RoPE is base-10000 (cfg10), the special case of lgqa's rope-scaled that band claim 1 proves == lblk-block-causal bit-for-bit. The llama3 theta-500000 + piecewise rope-scaling MSL emit is NOT yet on the GPU — it is the named next stone. The kernel runs single-thread (gid==0) serial to match the recipe fold order; parallelizing across query heads/tokens is a follow-up. Not yet wired to real llama3.2:3b GGUF weights (n_head=24, n_kv=8, head_dim=128) — the load path is already Form-native four-way (q4k/q6k/f16 dequant).
named_next_stones: emit the llama3 rope-scaling (rope_theta 500000 + piecewise wavelength scaling) to MSL so the GPU GQA block matches lgqa-block-causal under cfg-llama3 (not just cfg10) — the last numeric between this kernel and a real llama3.2 block
run this GQA block kernel over REAL llama3.2:3b GGUF weights at full width (n_q=24, n_kv=8, HD=128, d=3072) — the load path is already Form-native four-way
stack the GQA block kernel across the 28 real layers (the multi-layer GQA decode stack, the 3za lane with GQA blocks)
wire the full generation loop (tokenize-llama -> multi-layer GQA decode -> greedy argmax #3402) over real GGUF — the first end-to-end native llama token
